verses.text: God sends Jeremiah with an earthen bottle to the valley of Hinnom to proclaim judgment. A vivid sign is prepared. The place of their idolatry becomes the stage for their warning.
God sends Jeremiah with an earthen bottle to the valley of Hinnom to proclaim judgment. A vivid sign is prepared. The place of their idolatry becomes the stage for their warning.

He charges the kings with filling the place with innocent blood and building high places to burn their children to Baal. Their cruelty is monstrous. The sacrifice of children to idols is sin God never commanded nor imagined.

He charges the kings with filling the place with innocent blood and building high places to burn their children to Baal. Their cruelty is monstrous. The sacrifice of children to idols is sin God never commanded nor imagined.

He charges the kings with filling the place with innocent blood and building high places to burn their children to Baal. Their cruelty is monstrous. The sacrifice of children to idols is sin God never commanded nor imagined.

So this valley shall be 'the valley of slaughter,' and in the siege they shall eat their own children's flesh. Horror answers horror. The cruelty they practised will recoil upon them in dreadful measure.

So this valley shall be 'the valley of slaughter,' and in the siege they shall eat their own children's flesh. Horror answers horror. The cruelty they practised will recoil upon them in dreadful measure.

So this valley shall be 'the valley of slaughter,' and in the siege they shall eat their own children's flesh. Horror answers horror. The cruelty they practised will recoil upon them in dreadful measure.

So this valley shall be 'the valley of slaughter,' and in the siege they shall eat their own children's flesh. Horror answers horror. The cruelty they practised will recoil upon them in dreadful measure.
Jeremiah breaks the bottle before the men: 'Even so will I break this people and this city,' beyond mending. The sign is final. A vessel shattered cannot be repaired; so will their ruin be.
Jeremiah breaks the bottle before the men: 'Even so will I break this people and this city,' beyond mending. The sign is final. A vessel shattered cannot be repaired; so will their ruin be.
God will make the city as defiled Tophet, the houses polluted where they burned incense to the host of heaven. Their idolatry defiled all. The whole city is stained by the worship of false gods.
God will make the city as defiled Tophet, the houses polluted where they burned incense to the host of heaven. Their idolatry defiled all. The whole city is stained by the worship of false gods.

Returning from Tophet, Jeremiah stands in the temple court and declares the doom on Jerusalem for her stiff neck. He preaches openly. The faithful prophet proclaims judgment even in the heart of the city.
